items.mistake: Using sigmoid on every hidden layer in a deep feedforward network
items.fix: Use ReLU (or GELU/Swish) in hidden layers; reserve sigmoid for output probabilities or gates where (0, 1) is required.
items.why: Stacked sigmoid layers multiply σ′≤0.25 factors, starving early layers of gradient signal.
items.example: Wrong: 12-layer MLP with all Sigmoid(). Right: nn.ReLU() × 10 hidden + nn.Linear(…,1) + torch.sigmoid only at inference.

items.mistake: Assuming σ(x) can output exactly 0 or 1
items.fix: Treat outputs as open-interval (0, 1); clip only for numerical stability in loss (e.g. eps=1e-15), not as a mathematical claim.
items.why: For finite x, σ(x) is strictly between 0 and 1; exact zeros break log-loss unless clipped.
items.example: σ(1000) prints 1.0 in float32 but log(1−σ(1000)) still needs clip: p_hat = np.clip(sigmoid(z), 1e-15, 1-1e-15).

items.mistake: Applying BCE to already-sigmoided probabilities in PyTorch
items.fix: Pass raw logits to nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss(); call torch.sigmoid only when you need probabilities for display or thresholding.
items.why: Double sigmoid squashes gradients near 0 and 1 and computes the wrong loss.
items.example: Wrong: loss_fn(torch.sigmoid(logits), y). Right: loss_fn(logits, y) with BCEWithLogitsLoss().

items.mistake: Ignoring input scale so hidden pre-activations instantly saturate
items.fix: Standardize features, use sensible weight initialization (Xavier/He), and monitor histograms of pre-activation values during training.
items.why: Large |x| pushes σ into flat regions where σ′≈0 and learning stalls.
items.example: Unscaled pixel values 0–255 → first-layer z≈1200 → σ≈1.0, σ′≈0 for all samples in epoch 1.
